subject: Ecology
title: Habitat Islands and Reserves
description: One-minute skill: Habitat Islands and Reserves.

hook.text: A nature reserve surrounded by farmland follows the same ecological rules as an ocean island.

buildup.text: Island biogeography theory directly shaped how we design protected areas on land.

discovery.text: The SLOSS debate asked: is one big reserve better than several small ones of equal total area?

twist.text: The answer depends on the species—some need large ranges, others benefit from separate refuges.

climax.text: Connected reserves with corridors outperform isolated ones for most species.

punchline.text: Reserve design is island theory on dry land.

quiz.question: What does SLOSS stand for in conservation?
options: Single Large Or Several Small reserves
